message.content:
You are an independent blind verifier (verifier B) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-10). Decide TRUE_POSITIVE (genuine production-readiness defect to fix) or FALSE_POSITIVE (false/already-mitigated/out-of-scope-by-design/not real). Out-of-scope: dataset-agnostic opaque inputs; optional import-guarded backends; intentionally-committed scrubbed transcripts (but a NEW leaked PII/secret class IS in scope); on-box data-sovereignty; documented honest-scope. Read real code and REPRODUCE where testable (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python ...; "python -m pytest"; gh). Judge ONLY P10-MO-2.

FINDING P10-MO-2 (medium): from_records raises raw AttributeError on a non-mapping payload field (typed-error contract violation)
Location: src/assay_engine/provenance.py from_records
Claim: from_records is documented to raise a typed ProvenanceError on malformed input, but a record whose 'payload' field is a non-mapping (e.g. a list or string) makes it raise a raw AttributeError (e.g. calling .items()/.get() on a non-dict), violating the typed-error contract a caller relies on to distinguish corruption from a bug. VERIFY by feeding from_records a record with payload=[...] or payload="x" and checking the exception type.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The payload is just assigned, not iterated here. The `.items()`/`.get()` claim would happen during `verify_records` (hashing the payload). Let me check that.

content.text: Key line: line 66 in `ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__` calls `freeze_mapping(self.payload)`. That's where a non-mapping payload would fail. Let me look.

content.content:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:

129	def freeze_mapping(value: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FrozenDict:
130	    """Normalize a mapping field to a deep-immutable :class:`FrozenDict`.
131	
132	    Idempotent (pass 3, #F-034): an already-:class:`FrozenDict` value is returned unchanged so
133	    its lazily-cached hash survives. Re-wrapping (e.g. when ``ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__``
134	    freezes a payload that already holds FrozenDict values) would otherwise allocate a fresh
135	    FrozenDict with ``_hash=None`` and force an O(N·depth) re-hash on next use.
136	    """
137	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
138	        return value
139	    return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
140	
141	
142	def unfreeze(value: Any) -> Any:
143	    """Recursively convert a frozen structure back into plain JSON-serializable containers.
144	
145	    The inverse of :func:`freeze` for serialization: :class:`FrozenDict` → ``dict``,
146	    ``frozenset`` → sorted ``list`` (stable order for reproducible output), ``tuple`` →
147	    ``list``. Leaves are returned unchanged. ``json.dumps`` does not know :class:`FrozenDict`
148	    (it is a Mapping, not a ``dict`` subclass), so an operator serializing a deep-frozen public
149	    payload — e.g. ``GateReview.payload`` — must thaw it first (#141).
150	    """
151	    if isinstance(value, (FrozenDict, Mapping)):
152	        return {k: unfreeze(v) for k, v in value.items()}
153	    if isinstance(value, frozenset):
154	        # sort for reproducible output; when elements are not mutually orderable (mixed types),
155	        # fall back to a STABLE key — (type name, repr) — rather than raw set iteration order, which
156	        # is hash-seed/insertion dependent and would make serialized output non-reproducible across
157	        # processes (pass 4, #G-004).
158	        try:
159	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in sorted(value)]
160	        except TypeError:
161	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in sorted(value, key=lambda x: (type(x).__name__, repr(x)))]
162	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
163	        return [unfreeze(v) for v in value]
164	    return value
165

content.text: Confirmed: `freeze_mapping` calls `value.items()` at line 139. A non-mapping payload (list/string) would raise `AttributeError`. Now let me reproduce.

content.content:
[1, 2, 3] -> AttributeError : 'list' object has no attribute 'items'
'x' -> AttributeError : 'str' object has no attribute 'items'
42 -> AttributeError : 'int' object has no attribute 'items'

content.text:
The finding is reproduced. The reasoning is clear. The module explicitly documents (lines 82-88 for `_digest`, lines 278-281 for the missing-field case) a typed-error contract where malformed trail input must surface as `ProvenanceError`, not a raw exception. A non-mapping `payload` violates this with a raw `AttributeError`.

Let me confirm `ProvenanceError` is the documented contract and that the docstring of `from_records` references rebuilding/verifying (so callers reasonably catch `ProvenanceError`).

content.content:
243	def verify_records(
244	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
245	    *,
246	    secret: bytes | None = None,
247	) -> None:
248	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
249	
250	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
251	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
252	    """
253	    prev = _GENESIS
254	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
255	        if e.seq != i:
256	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
257	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
258	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
259	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
260	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
261	            raise ProvenanceError(
262	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
263	            )
264	        prev = e.entry_hash
265	
266

content.text:
Confirmed. The module's whole design around `from_records`/`verify_records` is to convert corruption into typed `ProvenanceError` (lines 256, 258, 261 for chain issues; lines 281 missing fields; lines 85-88 for unserializable payload). The codebase even has explicit pass-4 commentary (#G-012, #89) showing the intent that malformed trail records surface as `ProvenanceError` and never a raw exception.

The non-mapping payload case slips through because `ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__` runs `freeze_mapping` (calling `.items()`) before `from_records` ever reaches the typed-error-emitting `verify_records`/`_digest`. This is a genuine, consistent gap in the documented typed-error contract, reproduced with three distinct non-mapping payload types all raising raw `AttributeError`.

This is in scope (not dataset-agnostic opaque input, not import-guarded backend, not transcript/PII) and is a real production-readiness defect matching the existing convention the module already enforces elsewhere.

input.reasoning:
Reproduced. In /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/provenance.py, from_records (line 267) builds a ProvenanceEntry per record (line 282), whose __post_init__ (line 66) calls freeze_mapping(self.payload). freeze_mapping (src/assay_engine/_frozen.py:139) does value.items(), so a non-mapping payload raises a raw AttributeError before from_records ever reaches the typed-error-emitting verify_records/_digest.

Direct repro on branch harden/pass-10 with payload=[1,2,3] -> "'list' object has no attribute 'items'" (AttributeError), payload='x' -> "'str' object has no attribute 'items'", payload=42 -> "'int' object has no attribute 'items'". None raise ProvenanceError.

This violates the module's explicit, documented typed-error contract: from_records converts other malformed-input cases into ProvenanceError on purpose — missing fields (lines 278-281, citing #G-012, "must surface as the module's typed error, not a raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file covers this case too"), unserializable payloads in _digest (lines 82-88, #89, "the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception"), and chain/order/hash corruption (lines 256-263). A non-mapping payload is exactly the kind of corrupt-trail-file input the contract claims to cover, yet it leaks a raw AttributeError. A caller catching ProvenanceError to distinguish file corruption from a programming bug is broken by this gap.

In scope (not opaque dataset input, not an import-guarded backend, not a scrubbed transcript). Genuine production-readiness defect consistent with the convention the module already enforces everywhere else; fix is to validate payload is a Mapping (or wrap the freeze in a typed ProvenanceError) in from_records.

content.text: Verified TRUE_POSITIVE. The non-mapping payload case in `from_records` raises a raw `AttributeError` (reproduced with list, str, and int payloads), bypassing the module's explicit and well-documented typed-error contract that converts every other class of malformed/corrupt trail input into a typed `ProvenanceError`.
